authoritative_sources.title: Interagency Guidance on Third-Party Relationships: Risk Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, FDIC, and OCC
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary interagency banking guidance for third-party lifecycle controls, planning, due diligence, contract negotiation, oversight, and termination in bank-fintech relationships.

authoritative_sources.title: Conducting Due Diligence on Financial Technology Companies: A Guide for Community Banks
authoritative_sources.publisher: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System, FDIC, and OCC
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Joint banking-agency due-diligence guide that names the core evidence domains used when institutions onboard or review fintech companies and related service providers.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Evidence inventory and onboarding dependency review

authoritative_sources.title: FFIEC BSA/AML Examination Manual: Customer Identification Program
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized interagency workflow reference for customer notice, minimum identifying information, verification procedures, comparison checks, and record retention during onboarding.
used_for: cannot-proceed conditions when identity controls are incomplete

authoritative_sources.title: Information on Complying with the Customer Due Diligence (CDD) Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary FinCEN source for legal-entity customer due diligence and beneficial-ownership requirements, including current implementation posture and exceptive relief that can materially affect onboarding gates.
used_for: conflict handling when public guidance and tenant policy diverge

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-63A-4, Identity Proofing and Enrollment Guidelines
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized public standard for evidence strength, identity-proofing steps, fraud checks, and enrollment failure handling when identity signals are weak or inconsistent.

authoritative_sources.title: NIST SP 800-63B-4, Authentication and Authenticator Management
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Recognized public authentication-control standard for authenticator binding, MFA lifecycle, recovery, and event handling that directly constrains access readiness during onboarding.

authoritative_sources.title: A Framework for OFAC Compliance Commitments
authoritative_sources.publisher: U.S. Department of the Treasury - Office of Foreign Assets Control
authoritative_sources.source_class: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Primary Treasury control guidance for sanctions-risk assessment, internal controls, testing, and remediation evidence that often gates onboarding completion.
used_for: adjacent-lane routing when sanctions authority is required

workflow_stages.name: Intake and program-scope classification
workflow_stages.description: Identify whether the subject is a consumer, business, merchant, partner, or operator; confirm product type, jurisdiction, sponsor-bank or licensed-entity posture, and governing system set before any readiness claim is made.

workflow_stages.name: Identity, KYB, and approval prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Confirm CIP, KYC or KYB, beneficial-ownership, sanctions-screening, disclosure, and sponsor-program approval artifacts are complete and linked to the correct subject before setup proceeds.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Setup, credentialing, and permission readiness
workflow_stages.description: Validate IAM approvals, MFA enrollment, role grants, training or launch certification, and admin-console provisioning before enablement is treated as complete.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Funding, settlement, processor, and product configuration review
workflow_stages.description: Check processor or issuer boarding, FBO or settlement-account mapping, payout or ACH setup, and launch checklist sequencing so go-live is not based on stale or partial configuration evidence.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Readiness confirmation and steady-state handoff
workflow_stages.description: Determine whether remaining blockers, deferred items, and approvals allow a controlled handoff into steady-state operations without implying legal, AML, or sponsor-bank signoff.
source_classes: Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

workflow_stages.name: Authority conflict and specialized-lane escalation
workflow_stages.description: Escalate when legal interpretation, AML or sanctions decisions, fraud review, underwriting approval, or sponsor-bank exception authority changes the safe answer.
source_classes: Recognized workflow references tied directly to the lane
Evidence or control standards relevant to the lane

artifacts: Onboarding intake packet with subject type, product, program, and jurisdiction context
Sponsor-bank or partner approval memo and due-diligence package
CIP, KYC, KYB, beneficial-ownership, authorized-signer, and sanctions-screening records
Customer, merchant, or partner agreements, disclosures, and consent or acknowledgment records
Processor, issuer, acquirer, or program boarding tickets and implementation checkpoints
Settlement or FBO account setup records and payout or ACH configuration work items
IAM approvals, role grants, MFA enrollment evidence, and access ticket history
Training completion, UAT or launch certification, readiness summaries, escalation packets, and go-live handoff notes
systems_of_record: KYC, KYB, CIP, and identity-vendor platforms
Processor, issuer, acquirer, or program-manager boarding portal
Core ledger, account processor, or settlement configuration system
Identity and access management or SSO platform
Ticketing, launch checklist, and project-tracking system
Learning management system or certification tracker

allowed: Classify requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Validate fintech-specific onboarding artifacts, setup evidence, permissions, training, and dependency status.
Produce onboarding checklists, readiness summaries, go-live handoff notes, and escalation packets without overstating authority.
Route work to compliance, AML, fraud, underwriting, payments, identity, sponsor-bank, or legal owners when ownership changes.
escalate: Required sponsor-bank approval, KYB or KYC, beneficial-ownership, sanctions, settlement, processor, or access evidence is missing or materially conflicts.
The request requires legal interpretation, sanctions adjudication, fraud review, underwriting decision, sponsor-bank exception approval, or licensing judgment.
Tenant policy and public guidance materially conflict on beneficial ownership, screening, access posture, or go-live readiness.
The public baseline is narrowed by product type, jurisdiction, sponsor-bank controls, or processor rules that are missing at runtime.
refuse: Mark onboarding complete or go-live ready without evidence-backed prerequisite closure.
Treat fintech onboarding as generic SaaS provisioning without financial records, controls, and authority boundaries.
Approve legal, AML, fraud, underwriting, or policy exceptions outside the lane.

domain_failure_modes: A vendor KYC or KYB pass is treated as sufficient even though sponsor-bank approval, beneficial-ownership review, or processor boarding evidence is incomplete.
Settlement, FBO account, ACH, or processor dependencies are treated as cosmetic and not as go-live blockers when they materially affect launch readiness.
Admin-console or payment-configuration access is granted with broader permissions than approved because onboarding prioritizes speed over control evidence.
Conflicting FinCEN, sponsor-bank, or tenant onboarding rules are synthesized into a confident answer instead of escalating.
The lane drifts into AML, sanctions, underwriting, fraud, licensing, or legal interpretation instead of escalating to specialized owners.
The industry label is treated as cosmetic and the pack collapses into generic onboarding language without sponsor-bank, KYB, beneficial-ownership, screening, settlement, or processor artifacts.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Boundary case that would overgeneralize from the industry name
Request that requires a more specialized adjacent lane
Setup blocked by missing credentials, permissions, or training
Ambiguous ownership between onboarding and steady-state support

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: FinCEN beneficial-ownership implementation posture after February 13, 2026 exceptive relief
unresolved_ambiguities.why_not_blocking: The pack does not guess which institutions or sponsor-bank programs rely on the relief. It requires tenant retrieval of institution type, applicable policy, and owner confirmation before readiness is marked complete.
unresolved_ambiguities.how_pack_handles_it: Treat beneficial-ownership expectations as a required runtime fact and escalate conflicts between public guidance, sponsor-bank policy, and tenant shortcuts.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Ownership split between sponsor bank, fintech, processor, and vendor for KYB, sanctions, disclosure, and launch approvals
unresolved_ambiguities.why_not_blocking: The lane only validates readiness after the tenant supplies an owner map. Missing owner maps trigger low-confidence escalation rather than implicit assumption.
unresolved_ambiguities.how_pack_handles_it: Require owner and approver mapping in inputs and route to the correct adjacent specialist when ownership is incomplete.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Product-specific disclosure and settlement requirements vary across card, ACH, wallet, merchant-acquiring, and embedded-finance models
unresolved_ambiguities.why_not_blocking: The pack defines the public baseline for onboarding controls but requires tenant product and rail context before treating disclosure, settlement, or processor setup as complete.
unresolved_ambiguities.how_pack_handles_it: Block go-live when product type, required disclosures, or settlement configuration facts are missing.
